titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/28

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=282 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:51:51.354794+00 | translation moa=283 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:51:53.070457+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis . tam presentibus quam futuris . Grim filius guidonis caretarii de roxburgh’ salutem . Sciant omnes me concessisse et dedisse . et pro salute domini mei Willelmi de sumeruil . et pro anima mea . et pro anima patris mei . et matris mee . et omnium parentum meorum . totum toftum quod tenuit pater meus in berwic et ego prout eum tenui sicut in carta Willelmi predicti continetur . deo et ecclesie sancte marie de Melros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . hanc autem terram dedi eis in puram et perpetuam elemosinam cum omnibus libertatibus et pertinenciis suis intra burgum et extra burgum . tam libere et quiete . et plenarie . sicut aliqua alia elemosina liberius et quiecius possidetur . saluo seruicio domini . Willelmi de sumeruil et heredum eius quod illi de me et heredibus meis
debetur pro eadem terra . Et quia non habui proprium sigillum ad confirmandam et contestandam hanc meam donacionem roga
tu meo . magister Adam de Eccles . sigillum suum in hac mea presenti carta apposuit . Hiis testibus Magistro
Adam de Eccles … capellano . Ricardo de Dunst’ . Rogero filio Henrici . Randulfo de cell’r . Reinaldo de Hoy
land . Willelmo clerico … clerico . …[?E]theolf portman . Gocellino lunoc . Willelmo franc’ . Willelmo albo .
Iohanne Nigro Willelmo Heem .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church, as well present as future, Grim son of Guy the carter of Roxburgh, greeting. Let all men know that I have granted and given, and for the wellbeing of my lord William de Somerville, and for my own soul, and for the soul of my father, and of my mother, and of all my ancestors, the whole toft that my father held in Berwick, and just as I have held it, as it is set out in the charter of the aforesaid William, to God and the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God there, this land, however, have I given to them in pure and perpetual alms with all its liberties and pertinents within the burgh and outside the burgh, so freely and quietly and fully as any other alms is freely and quietly possessed, reserving the service of the lord William de Somerville and his heirs which is owed to him from me and my heirs in return for the same land. And because I do not have my own seal for affirming and attesting this my donation, Master Adam of Eccles at my request has placed his own seal on this my present charter. With these witnesses, Master Adam of Eccles … chaplain, Richard of Dunston, Roger son of Henry, Ralph the cellarer, Reynaud of Hoyland, William the clerk … the clerk, … Edolf Portman, Goscelin Lunnock, William Francis, William White, John Black, William Heem.
